t_ch: 向你致敬，{NICKNAME}教官。哈！哈！哈！
t_en: I salute you, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}. Bwahaha!

t_ch: 记住，你要击败四十名铁卫才算通过演练！
t_en: Remember, you have to defeat forty Silvermane Guards to pass this drill!

t_ch: 杰帕德戍卫官也不知道什么时候会加入战斗，你可要小心了！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Captain Gepard could join the fray any time, so please be careful! Bwahaha!

t_ch: 去吧，我看好你，{NICKNAME}教官！
t_en: Go on then. I believe in you, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 居然在这里偷懒？让我逮住了吧！
t_en: Are you slacking off here? I've caught you now!

t_ch: 但、但是我们根本不可能打赢那位教官啊……
t_en: B—But there's no way we can beat that drillmaster...

t_ch: 还要等多久……
t_en: How long am I gonna have to wait...

t_ch: 嘘！我感觉有人要来了，准备作战！
t_en: Shh! I feel like someone's coming. Get ready to fight!

t_ch: （大门紧闭着……）
t_en: (The door is closed...)

t_ch: 用力敲门。
t_en: Knock loudly on it.

t_ch: 大门纹丝不动，门后没有任何声响。
t_en: The door doesn't budge. No sound comes from behind it.

t_ch: …该不该继续尝试呢？
t_en: ...Should you keep knocking?

t_ch: （大门依旧紧闭着……）
t_en: (The door remains tightly closed...)

t_ch: 更用力地敲门。
t_en: Knock even more loudly on it.

t_ch: 大门依旧纹丝不动，但里面似乎传来了什么动静…只持续了一瞬间。
t_en: The door still doesn't budge, but for just a split second... you think you can hear faint noises from within.

t_ch: 也许你该再坚持一下？
t_en: Maybe you should keep at it a little longer?

t_ch: （大门依旧紧闭着……）
t_en: (The door remains tightly closed...)

t_ch: 更、更用力地敲门。
t_en: Knock even MORE loudly.

t_ch: 大门没有回应，但「少校」嘹亮的全域广播传入了你的耳朵——
t_en: There's no response, but you hear the Major's loud region-wide broadcast...

t_ch: 喂喂，{NICKNAME}教官！你可要想清楚了，杰帕德戍卫官说不定就在那扇大门后面休整哦？
t_en: Hey, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! Do you know what you're doing? Maybe Captain Gepard is resting behind that door!

t_ch: 主动去招惹他的话，<color=#dbc291ff>战斗说不定会变得异常棘手……</color>
t_en: If you purposefully go bother him, <color=#dbc291ff>your fight might get real tricky...</color>

t_ch: 不过「擒什么先擒王」这种说法，你肯定听过吧？如果你真的能战胜全盛状态的银鬃铁卫戍卫官，那这次演练也就没有什么拖下去的必要了。
t_en: "If you want to kill a snake, chop off its head" — ever heard that one before? If you really can defeat the captain of the Silvermane Guards at his full strength, then there's no need for you to drag this drill on.

t_ch: 你会怎么选，{NICKNAME}教官？
t_en: What'll it be, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 让你们见识下什么叫斩首行动。
t_en: I'll show you how it's done with capturing the ringleader.

t_ch: …不愧是你，有胆魄！
t_en: ...I expected no less from you. What bravery!

t_ch: 铁卫，开门！
t_en: Guards, open up!

t_ch: 我还是乖乖去打铁卫吧…
t_en: I think I'll be good and take the Guards instead...

t_ch: …还有一句话叫「识时务者为俊杰」！
t_en: ...There's another saying: "A wise man adapts to circumstance."

t_ch: 明智的选择，{NICKNAME}教官！
t_en: A wise choice, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 喔唷，{NICKNAME}！你居然真来了！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! I can't believe you are here!

t_ch: 我本来都不指望了——毕竟你之前已经帮了我们那么多，再提要求就有点蛮不讲理的意思了。
t_en: I didn't think you would come. After all, you have offered us a lot of help. I'd be imposing on you if I ask anything more from you.

t_ch: 我享受和裂界战斗。
t_en: I enjoy fighting the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 唷，血气方刚！有我年轻时的派头，哈哈哈！
t_en: Wow, I admire your fighting spirit! You remind me of myself when I was young, hahaha!

t_ch: 有奖励都好说。
t_en: As long as I'll be rewarded, I'm open to any missions.

t_ch: 那是当然，这怎么可能让你白出力呢？于情于理都说不过去啊。
t_en: Of course, you will. How can we make you work for nothing?

t_ch: 惩奸除恶，义不容辞。
t_en: I'll do my best to punish and eliminate the evil.

t_ch: 唷，大义凛然！有我年轻时的派头，哈哈哈！
t_en: Wow, you're so righteous! You remind me of myself when I was young, hahaha!

t_ch: 话说回来，这「残响回廊」……
t_en: By the way, this Corridor of Fading Echoes...

t_ch: 荒凉，比我想象中还要荒凉。我只记得十几年前，贝洛伯格的北方还没有破败到这种程度。
t_en: It's way more desolate than I imagined. A decade or so ago, the northern region of Belobog was in a better state than this.

t_ch: 不过迟早有一天，我们要把这些残垣断壁全都修整一遍，把那群怪物统统赶出这片城区。
t_en: I believe we'll repair the ruins and get rid of the monsters one day.

t_ch: 听好，{NICKNAME}，这次的作战目标很简单，<color=#dbc291ff>全灭「残响回廊」里出现的裂界怪物。</color>
t_en: Listen up, {NICKNAME}. The goal of this operation is simple: <color=#dbc291ff>Destroy all the Fragmentum monsters in the Corridor of Fading Echoes</color>.

t_ch: 威廉应该跟你提过「照雪灯炉」了，对吧？技术部之前给我科普了那玩意的用法。我凭记忆再给你复述一次，听好了。
t_en: William must have told you about the Snowshine Lamp, right? The Technology Division has taught me how to use these things before. I'll try and lay it out for you from memory, so listen up.

t_ch: 「照雪灯炉」由两部分组成，第一部分是地髓驱动的<color=#dbc291ff>「热能核心」</color>。别看这小玩意不起眼，里面可是蕴含了巨大的能量。
t_en: Snowshine Lamps are made from two parts, one of which is the <color=#dbc291ff>thermic core</color> driven by Geomarrow. Don't underestimate this little gizmo, as it's packing some real power inside.

t_ch: 第二部分就是<color=#dbc291ff>「基座」。只要把「热能核心」安插到「基座」上，就可以启动「照雪灯炉」，放射出足以削弱裂界怪物的能量场</color>。
t_en: The second part is the <color=#dbc291ff>base. Slotting the thermic core into the base activates the Snowshine Lamp and radiates an energy field, which can weaken the Fragmentum monsters</color>.

t_ch: 不过，「热能核心」的结构非常不稳定，在长期无人看管的情况下可能会自行脱落，<color=#dbc291ff>甚至被怪物藏到难以寻找的位置……</color>
t_en: But the thermic core's structure is very unstable and can fall out over time if nobody is monitoring it — <color=#dbc291ff>and may even be carted off and hidden by these monsters...</color>

t_ch: 如果你想借用「照雪灯炉」的机制来降低作战的负荷，那就多留意一下散在它处的「热能核心」吧。
t_en: If you want to use the Snowshine Lamps to reduce your load in combat, then pay close attention to its loose thermic core.

t_ch: 噢，对了，现在这风雪可不小…战斗的时候要小心，身体被冻僵就不好办了。
t_en: Oh yeah — the snow's really coming down now. Be careful when you're fighting. You really don't want to come down with frostbite.

t_ch: 去击垮它们吧，{NICKNAME}。有我在这里把守，不必担心背后。
t_en: Go and defeat them, {NICKNAME}. I'll keep watch here, so don't worry about guarding your back.

t_ch: 只要<color=#dbc291ff>把热能核心和底座组合起来</color>，照雪灯炉就能发挥出效果了。
t_en: As long as <color=#dbc291ff>the thermic core is slotted into its base</color>, the Snowshine Lamps will fire up.

t_ch: 启动灯炉，然后<color=#dbc291ff>把那些杂碎吸引到能量场里</color>逐个击破吧。
t_en: Fire up the lamp, then <color=#dbc291ff>lure those scums into its energy field</color> to destroy them one by one.

t_ch: 唔…这颗核心怎么被丢到那么远的角落去了？也不知道是谁干的……
t_en: Whoa... How did this core make it all the way over there? Who did that...

t_ch: 这么大的雪，我建议你每一步都谨慎行事，{NICKNAME}。<color=#dbc291ff>绕过巡逻的怪物，去把核心捡回来吧。</color>
t_en: In a snowstorm this bad, I advise you proceed carefully with every step, {NICKNAME}. <color=#dbc291ff>Kite around any patrolling monsters, and retrieve the core.</color>

t_ch: 这照雪灯炉的效果的确不同凡响，应该多加利用才是。
t_en: Snowshine Lamps are simply incredible. We really should deploy them more often.

t_ch: 干得好，{NICKNAME}！干净利落，是你一贯的风格。
t_en: Well done, {NICKNAME}! Being straightforward has always been your style.

t_ch: 周围已经没有怪物活动的迹象了，我猜这回廊里应该能暂时消停一阵。
t_en: There are no more traces of monsters in the surroundings. The corridor should be peaceful from now on.

t_ch: …不过裂界的影响可没这么容易根除，离那些家伙卷土重来也只是时间问题。到时候我可能还要麻烦你来帮忙。
t_en: ...Yet, the effects of Fragmentum are here to stay. It's only a matter of time before they make a comeback. I might need your help again when the time comes.

t_ch: 辛苦你跟那个叫威廉的联络员保持联系了。那小伙子虽然说话不太利索，但办事靠谱的很，希望你多担待。
t_en: I hope you'll keep in touch with William. That boy might not be articulate, but he is very reliable. Please be more patient with him.

t_ch: 这些东西你拿去吧，就当是我雇你干这趟苦差的报酬。保重，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: These are for you. You can treat them as my rewards for you. Take care, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: （这一拨裂界怪物应该都清理完了…回去跟奥列格汇报吧。）
t_en: (This group of monsters should be done for... Go back and report to Oleg.)

t_ch: 回去向奥列格汇报
t_en: Go back and report to Oleg.

t_ch: 唷，来这边！
t_en: Hey, come here!

t_ch: 唷，又见面了，{NICKNAME}！我就知道，你这热心肠的家伙肯定不会袖手旁观的。
t_en: Here we are again, {NICKNAME}! I knew someone as kind as you wouldn't watch the trouble from the sidelines.

t_ch: 如我所料，裂界肯定不会就此安分下来。北边的城墙外不知道还潜藏着多少怪物，时刻准备好侵入回廊。
t_en: As I expected, the Fragmentum would not be willing to back off so easily. There must be countless monsters lurking outside the northern wall, waiting to invade the corridor.

t_ch: 你抬头看，这糟糕透顶的天气…空气里还有一股子裂界的腐败味道……
t_en: Look at this horrible weather... The stinking smell of the Fragmentum permeates the air...

t_ch: 我有种预感，{NICKNAME}——这次我们要面对的敌人肯定更加难缠。不过好在技术部已经提前部署好了照雪灯炉，对我们的行动有很大帮助。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I have a feeling we'll be up against even tougher enemies this time. Luckily, the Technology Division has made sure all the Snowshine Lamps are ready — it is a tremendous help to our action.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>找到能源核心，启动照雪灯炉，在它们的加持下干掉敌人吧。</color>记住，扫荡要彻底，裂界里的杂碎一个不留！
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Find the energy core and activate the Snowshine Lamp. Then, get rid of the enemies with the help of the light.</color> Remember to do a thorough job and spare none of the Fragmentum monsters!

t_ch: 这个关口就由我来把守。前方的扫荡工作就交给你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'll stay to guard this pass. I'll count on you to finish up the task, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 干得好，{NICKNAME}！在你的帮助下，咱们又拿下一场大胜！
t_en: Awesome, {NICKNAME}! We've achieved another great victory with your help!

t_ch: 周围已经没有怪物活动的迹象了，我猜这回廊里应该能暂时消停一阵。
t_en: There are no more traces of monsters in the surroundings. The corridor should be peaceful from now on.

t_ch: 不过，即便我们已经完成了两次扫荡…我猜裂界的怪潮还是不会善罢甘休。毫无疑问，我们要面临的是一场持久战，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Even though we've carried out two raids, I don't think it's enough to send them home. Without a doubt, we're facing a protracted war, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 还请你和威廉保持通信，如果回廊里又出了什么状况，他肯定是第一个知道消息的。
t_en: I hope you'll keep in touch with William. If anything happens here, William will be the first to know.

t_ch: 这些东西你拿去吧，就当是我雇你干这趟苦差的报酬。保重，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: These are for you. You can treat them as my rewards for you. Take care, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: （那堆怪物后面有一座照雪灯炉…也许可以避免正面蛮干，绕后解决它们？希望还能捡到多余的核心，点燃那座灯炉……）
t_en: (There's a Snowshine Lamp behind that mob of monsters... Instead of facing them head-on, maybe we can skirt around the side and take them out? I hope we can get that extra core and fire up the Snowshine Lamp...)

t_ch: 唷，又见面了，{NICKNAME}！威廉告诉我你正在路上的时候，你都想象不到我有多激动！
t_en: Here we are again, {NICKNAME}! You could have no idea how thrilled I was when William told me you were on your way here!

t_ch: 之前的两次作战，你的表现真是惊人——铁卫里的小年轻都在疯传你的事迹呢。依我看，你在部队里的名望很快就要超过我了，可喜可贺！
t_en: Your performance in the last two raids was outstanding! The young Silvermane Guards can't stop talking about you. In my opinion, your reputation in the troop will soon surpass mine. Congratulations!

t_ch: 轻轻松松，滴汗未流。
t_en: It's a walk in the park to me.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，年轻就是好啊！从远处欣赏你作战的英姿，对我这把老骨头来说也算是种享受！
t_en: Hahaha, it's good to be young! For an old-timer like me, it's a treat to watch you in action from afar!

t_ch: 我还在等你出手。
t_en: I'm still waiting for you to get your hands dirty.

t_ch: 我这把年纪，上前线硬拼已经有点力不从心了。我还是从远处欣赏你作战的英姿吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Fighting at the front lines is a tall order for someone my age. I'll stay here to watch you in action, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 话说回来…你能感受到吧，{NICKNAME}？这空气里的压迫感……
t_en: By the way... Can you feel the oppression in the air here, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 到了这个地步，我也没什么好叮嘱你的了。去战斗吧，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: At this point, I have no more advice to give you. Just put your best foot forward, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 不用担心背后，这里有我在。用上你能利用的所有资源，杀它们个片甲不留！
t_en: I have your back. Use all the available resources and annihilate them!

t_ch: （齿轮栈桥的桥臂上也设置了照雪灯炉……）
t_en: (There's also a Snowshine Lamp on the trestle of the gear bridge...)

t_ch: （或许能利用这点来清理裂界怪物？试试看吧。）
t_en: (Perhaps we can use this to clear out all the Fragmentum monsters? Let's give it a try.)

t_ch: 难以置信…你凭一己之力就把它们干得落花流水，而且自身毫发无损。
t_en: This is unbelievable... You've single-handedly beaten them to a pulp without being injured.

t_ch: 你是贝洛伯格的传奇，{NICKNAME}。你不单存护了这座城市的当下，还为保住它的未来尽了一份力。
t_en: You're the legend of Belobog, {NICKNAME}. Not only have you preserved the present of this city, but you've also contributed to its future.

t_ch: 夸人的事我不太会，可能还是那个叫威廉的小伙比较擅长。不过，我还是得让你知道，贝洛伯格所有人都欠你一句谢谢。
t_en: William might be better than I at giving compliments. Still, I must let you know that every citizen of Belobog owes you one.

t_ch: 不说这些了，聊点开心的吧——比如你的奖励。拿着，{NICKNAME}！这些都是你靠实力挣来的。
t_en: Let's talk about something positive, like your rewards. Please have this, {NICKNAME}! You have earned this.

t_ch: *电波滤音*为了你的安全，我帮你远程启动了「回响发生装置」。
t_en: *electronic voice* For the sake of your safety, I've remotely activated the Reverberation Generator.

t_ch: *电波滤音*异象中的隐形怪物非常敏锐，请小心回避，不要被怪物先发制人。接下来何时启动装置，就交由你自己判断了。
t_en: *electronic voice* The invisible monsters affected by the anomaly are extremely keen, so be careful and don't let them catch you off guard. As for when to activate the generator next, I'll leave that up to you.

t_ch: （确实有进食的痕迹，并且像是四足生物，还需要一些线索。）
t_en: (There are indeed signs of feeding here, and it looks like it's from a four-legged creature. I need more clues.)

t_ch: （这里有进食痕迹，难道说真的是一种*野生生物*引起了异象？）
t_en: (Here, some signs of feeding. Is this anomaly really caused by some sort of "wild creature?")

t_ch: （这两处线索都指向了海滩更深处，到那边调查看看吧。）
t_en: (Both clues lead to deeper parts of the beach. I should investigate there.)

t_ch: *电波滤音*我这边一无所获。您似乎发现了些线索，请继续调查，我随后赶到。
t_en: *electronic voice* I've found nothing on my end, but it seems you've found some clues. Please keep it up. I'll be arriving soon.

t_ch: *电波滤音*这附近怪物数量太多，<color=#dbc291ff>请直接冲刺到浮梯处，不必恋战！</color>
t_en: *electronic voice* There are way too many monsters here. <color=#dbc291ff>Just sprint straight to the levitator and don't engage with them!</color>

t_ch: （好像是次元扑满的声音，赶紧跟上去。）
t_en: (That sounds like a Warp Trotter. I should hurry up and track it down.)

t_ch: （扑满应该是逃进行医市集里了，还得继续向前才能找到它……）
t_en: (It must have escaped into the Healer's Market. I've got to keep moving to find it...)

t_ch: （这次不能让它再逃走了。）
t_en: (I mustn't let it get away this time.)

t_ch: （在这里能听到扑满的声音，应该就在这附近了…）
t_en: (I can hear the Trotter again. It must be somewhere close by...)

t_ch: （剩下的扑满应该都在积玉坊的广场上了，一口气全部抓住吧。）
t_en: (All the other Trotters should be at the plaza at Trove of Verdure. Let's catch them all at once.)

t_ch: （次元扑满在危急时刻能够跨维度逃跑，必须要赶在离开前击败它。）
t_en: (Warp Trotters will escape to other dimensions in times of distress. I must defeat them before they get away.)

t_ch: *电波滤音*我们已经侦测到异变的范围在不断缩小，这一切努力果然没有白费。
t_en: *electronic voice* The anomaly's impact area has become smaller. Looks like we're making progress.

t_ch: *电波滤音*加油，我们胜利在望。
t_en: *electronic voice* Keep at it! We're doing great!

t_ch: *电波滤音*没错，就是这个方向，次元扑满向这个方向逃了。
t_en: *electronic voice* Yep, we're on the right track. That Warp Trotter is fleeing in this direction.

t_ch: *电波滤音*我们地衡司的人不应该早就把这里封锁住了吗？怎么回事……
t_en: *electronic voice* Wait, I thought my colleagues in the Realm-Keeping Commission had already sealed off this area. What's going on here...?

t_ch: 还算顺利，这应该就是最后一只扑满了。
t_en: Things are looking good. This must be the last of those Trotters.

t_ch: 我们已经进入到事发地点，这附近的怪物都受到「云吟术」异象的影响隐去了身形。
t_en: We've made it to the site where the anomaly hit. All the monsters in this area are under the influence of the Cloudhymn Magic and are invisible.

t_ch: 但是根据地衡司的猜测，引起异象的元凶就藏在这里。
t_en: The Realm-Keeping Commission believes that the culprit for this anomaly is hiding here somewhere.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>使用「回响发生装置」</color>应该就能锁定隐身怪物的位置，请酌情使用。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>Reverberation Generator</color> should help locate invisible monsters. Please use it wisely.

t_ch: 接下来我会调查另一个方向，就麻烦{NICKNAME}你调查海滩了。
t_en: I'll be investigating the other direction next. Please help me check out the beach, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 希望你一路安全，保持远程联系，我们一会碰头。
t_en: Be careful. We'll stay in remote contact, and I'll catch up with you later.

t_ch: *电波滤音*对了，地衡司提前在海滩上放置了些饵食。如果「云吟术」的异象总是与食物有关的话，饵食附近会有所痕迹，能麻烦你去确认一下吗？
t_en: *electronic voice* By the way, the Realm-Keeping Commission has placed some bait on the beach. If the Cloudhymn Magic is really linked to food, you may find something near the bait. Could you go check it out?

t_ch: *电波滤音*我们模仿捕猎生物的方式，将饵食放在沙滩的草丛旁边，希望对你有所帮助。
t_en: *electronic voice* We decided to adopt a hunter's approach — we left some bait near the grass at the beach. Let's hope this will help you.

t_ch: （那是…次元扑满？难道那些食物是……）
t_en: (Is that a... Warp Trotter? Could the food be...)

t_ch: （总之先解决掉怪物，抓住次元扑满！）
t_en: (Anyway, I should focus on the monsters and catch the Warp Trotter!)

t_ch: 看来我来晚了一步，异象消失了。
t_en: Looks like I'm too late. The anomaly has disappeared.

t_ch: 不过这一趟收获不少，至少我们搞清楚了异象的元凶——次元扑满。
t_en: But at least we've figured out something. Turns out those Warp Trotters are the ones behind the anomaly.

t_ch: …扑满会云吟术？
t_en: ...Can Trotters use cloudhymn magic?

t_ch: 应该不会，不如说它模拟了「云吟术」。
t_en: I don't think so. It would be better to say they've somehow learned to imitate cloudhymn magic.

t_ch: …肯定又是解释不清的原理！
t_en: ...As for how they caused the anomaly, it's still a mystery.

t_ch: 毕竟，对于这种能跨维度的生物，我们也谈不上知根知底。
t_en: After all, we don't know much about these dimension-leaping creatures.

t_ch: 我有个大胆的猜测——它也许是模拟了「云吟术」。
t_en: My best guess is that maybe they've somehow imitated cloudhymn magic.

t_ch: 总之，关于异象的调查，我们又进了一步，接下来地衡司会以「次元扑满」为方向继续展开调查，这一切都要归功于您。
t_en: Anyway, we've made progress in the investigation. Thanks to your discoveries, the Realm-Keeping Commission will be looking into those Warp Trotters next.

t_ch: 如果您有心帮忙到底的话，我也会在丹鼎司等您的。拿着吧，这是您应得的酬劳，感谢您协助地衡司完成了本次调查。我们该回去了。
t_en: Meet me at the Alchemy Commission if you're willing to help us all the way through. But for now, please take this reward as a token of our appreciation for all your assistance. We should be heading back.

t_ch: *电波滤音*行医市集处居民已经疏散了，现在就是你大显神威的时候了。
t_en: *electronic voice* The residents at the Healer's Market have been evacuated. Now it's time to show off what you're made of!

t_ch: *电波滤音*我们已经侦查到行医市集附近有着大量怪物聚集，应该都是随着「云吟术」的异象一起出现的，想必你已经有经验了。
t_en: *electronic voice* We've detected a lot of monsters near the Healer's Market. They must have manifested with the cloudhymn magic anomalies, but I'm pretty sure you know well how to handle them.

t_ch: *电波滤音*还得提醒你一次，<color=#dbc291ff>「回响发生装置」</color>能够发现怪物的位置，请酌情使用。
t_en: *electronic voice* As another reminder, the <color=#dbc291ff>Reverberation Generator</color> will help you locate the monsters. Please use it wisely.

t_ch: *电波滤音*记住，<color=#dbc291ff>不必恋战</color>，目标一直是次元扑满。我们保持远程联系，请一路小心。
t_en: *electronic voice* And just a heads-up, <color=#dbc291ff>don't engage those monsters</color>. Our main focus is the Warp Trotter. We'll stay in contact remotely. Please be careful.

t_ch: （是上一次逃走的次元扑满，是被饵食吸引到这里来了吗？）
t_en: (Did the Warp Trotter that escaped last time get attracted here by the bait?)

t_ch: （要追上去的话，只能解决眼前的怪物了……）
t_en: (I've got to deal with the monsters before I can catch that Trotter...)

t_ch: （看我先下手为强）
t_en: (Seize the upper hand and strike first!)

t_ch: （趁乱直接抓住扑满）
t_en: (Take advantage of the chaos and catch the Trotter directly.)

t_ch: （糟糕，刚才的战斗应该是惊动了扑满，它逃向了道路一侧。）
t_en: (Oh no, the fight just now alerted the Trotter. Now it's running off to the side of the road.)

t_ch: （小心跟上去，要确保扑满进到行医市集里。）
t_en: (Follow it carefully and make sure it runs towards the Healer's Market.)

t_ch: 无路可逃的次元扑满在你的面前瑟瑟发抖，它没有想到「寻味丹鼎司之旅」竟是它的穷途末路。噢，可怜的次元扑满。
t_en: Cornered and trapped at a dead end, the Warp Trotter trembles before you. Obviously, it didn't expect its "gourmet-crawl across the Alchemy Commission" to end like this. Oh, what a poor little guy.

t_ch: 请问，就在当下，你的心中是否产生了一丝不忍的情绪？或者说你早已经习惯了心如寒铁，没有任何事情能够让你动摇？
t_en: At this moment, are you feeling any trace of pity for this poor creature? Or has your heart become as cold as iron, impervious to any emotion?

t_ch: 早抓住早结束，差不多得了。
t_en: I'll just catch it and get this over with.

t_ch: 这不过就是一次任务罢了，「不忍的情绪」能带来什么？差评如潮的续集而已。
t_en: What use is feeling "traces of pity" for the work you do? It's useless. It's just like how a classic movie doesn't need a terrible sequel.

t_ch: 我觉得我应该放走次元扑满。
t_en: I think I should let this poor little guy go.

t_ch: 放走次元扑满，这意味着丹鼎司里随时可能会再出现异常…更重要的是，也许你会完成不了任务目标，这样真的可以吗？
t_en: Letting it go means that anomalies may happen manifest in the Alchemy Commission again... and more importantly, you won't be able to complete your task. Are you really sure you want to do this?

t_ch: *电波滤音*…能听到我的声音吗？
t_en: *electronic voice* ...Can you hear me?

t_ch: *电波滤音*…你开着「回响发生装置」吗？小心，你背后好像有什么接近了！
t_en: *electronic voice* ...Do you have the Reverberation Generator on? Watch out! Something is approaching you from behind!

t_ch: 功夫不负有心人，这只次元扑满终于被我们抓住了！…主要是被你抓住了！
t_en: Finally, we've caught this Warp Trotter... or rather, you've caught it!

t_ch: 估计这里的异象很快也会结束。
t_en: I think the anomaly here will subside soon.

t_ch: 「追猎行动」成功？
t_en: So, the "Hunting Operation" was a success?

t_ch: 非常成功！
t_en: A great success!

t_ch: …这行动，简单到令人发笑。
t_en: ...This "operation" was hilariously easy.

t_ch: 我知道您想的是什么。
t_en: I understand what you must be thinking.

t_ch: 不过，仅凭一只次元扑满是没有办法在如此大范围内制造异象的，你懂我的意思吧？
t_en: However, it's impossible for only one Warp Trotter to create such a large-scale anomaly, if you understand what I mean.

t_ch: …还有很多次元扑满的意思？
t_en: ...So, there are more Warp Trotters out there?

t_ch: 没错，还有很多……
t_en: Yeah, a lot more...

t_ch: …我不明白你的意思。
t_en: I don't understand what you mean.

t_ch: 还有很多次元扑满需要我们捕获。
t_en: We still have many more Warp Trotters to catch.

t_ch: …突然有点不想干了。
t_en: ...I suddenly feel like quitting.

t_ch: 等等，先听我说完，还有很多次元扑满需要我们捕获。
t_en: Not so fast. We still need to catch a lot more Warp Trotters.

t_ch: 但好消息还是有的！下一次，就在下一次，我们能够将它们一网打尽！
t_en: But here's the good news! We'll be able to catch them all in our next operation!

t_ch: 如果您有心帮忙到底的话，我也会在丹鼎司等您的。拿着吧，这是您应得的酬劳，感谢您协助地衡司完成了本次调查。
t_en: Meet me at the Alchemy Commission if you're willing to help us all the way. But for now, please take this reward as a token of appreciation for all you've done.

t_ch: 「我笑扑满无谋，孽物少智。若我用兵，在狭隘的出口埋伏一只隐形怪物。我纵然不被所擒，也必然损耗巨大。」
t_en: "I laugh at the Trotter's poor tactics and the abomination's lacking guile. Were I them, I'd set an invisible creature at the narrow pass, and catch the hunters by surprise."

t_ch: 这行动已经很不容易啦，都怪您太厉害了。
t_en: This operation is far from simple. Maybe it's just because you're too skilled.

t_ch: *电波滤音*地衡司已经封锁了积玉坊附近的区域，你只要<color=#dbc291ff>专心猎捕扑满</color>就行！
t_en: *electronic voice* The Realm-Keeping Commission has sealed off the area near the Trove of Verdure. Just <color=#dbc291ff>focus on tracking down the Trotter</color>!

t_ch: *电波滤音*…你还记得<color=#dbc291ff>「回响发生装置」</color>吗？能够发现隐形怪物的位置，请酌情使用。
t_en: *electronic voice* Remember that the <color=#dbc291ff>Reverberation Generator</color> will help you locate invisible monsters. Please use it wisely.

t_ch: *电波滤音*请记住，<color=#dbc291ff>我们的目标是次元扑满</color>，很多的次元扑满。
t_en: *electronic voice* Remember, <color=#dbc291ff>our main focus is the Warp Trotters</color>, and I mean the entire lot of them.

t_ch: *电波滤音*我们保持远程联系，祝你狩猎愉快。
t_en: *electronic voice* We'll stay in contact remotely. Good hunting.

t_ch: （集装箱里好像有什么声音…不出意料的话，应该是次元扑满的声音……）
t_en: (I hear some noise coming from the container... There could be a Warp Trotter in there...)

t_ch: （应该是有次元扑满被关在里面了…难道说集装箱里存着什么食物吗？）
t_en: (Looks like a Warp Trotter got itself locked inside... Maybe there's some food stored in that container?)

t_ch: （果然不出所料。）
t_en: (As I suspected.)

t_ch: *电波滤音*我们侦测到最后一只次元扑满逃走了。
t_en: *electronic voice* We've detected that the last Warp Trotter has escaped.

t_ch: *电波滤音*希望你已经注意到了它逃走的方向，千万不要让它再逃走了!
t_en: *electronic voice* Make sure you keep an eye on which direction it's heading and don't let it get away again!

t_ch: *电波滤音*「牧场行动」成败在此一举！
t_en: *electronic voice* It's up to you to complete this "Herding Operation."

t_ch: 不出意外，次元扑满总是能抓住一切机会狐假虎威，这毕竟也是它的可爱之处。
t_en: Unsurprisingly, Warp Trotters will always try to trick you at every opportunity, but that's part of what makes them so adorable.

t_ch: 没有什么好犹豫的，你都已经捕获了那么多扑满，不可能在面对最后一只时大发善心…等等，你不会是真的想放过它吧？
t_en: There's no need to hesitate. You've already caught so many Trotters, and you're not planning on letting the last one go... Wait a minute, you're not really considering it, are you?

t_ch: 我从来没有犹豫过，现在就抓。
t_en: I've never hesitated before. I'll catch it now.

t_ch: 很好，这次不会让它再跑了，上吧！
t_en: Good, the Trotter won't get away this time. Catch it!

t_ch: …万一我真的想放过它呢？
t_en: ...What if I really want to let it go?

t_ch: 如果你真的放过了这只扑满，这任务就会完成不了，你就得假装自己好像没有参与过「牧场行动」一样，再挑战一次。
t_en: If you let this Trotter go, you'll fail this task. Then, you'll have to pretend you didn't even participate in the "Herding Operation" before starting everything all over again.

t_ch: …就在这里让它结束吧！
t_en: ...This has to end here!

t_ch: 现场所有的次元扑满已经被地衡司管理。「牧场行动」大获成功，这都是你的功劳。
t_en: Thanks to you, all the Warp Trotters are now under the supervision of the Realm-Keeping Commission. The "Herding Operation" has been a huge success.

t_ch: 感觉比前几次好玩？
t_en: It felt more fun than the previous hunts, I guess?

t_ch: 是吗，您玩得开心就好。
t_en: Oh, really? Glad to know that.

t_ch: 这也太没有意思了！
t_en: This was not fun at all!

t_ch: 很抱歉，不过这就是工作。
t_en: I'm sorry, but when it comes to work, it's not always about having fun.

t_ch: 相信这次行动结束之后，「模拟云吟术」引起的骚动会暂时平息吧。
t_en: I believe our operations will settle the commotion caused by the imitated cloudhymn magic for a while.

t_ch: 不过，我现在还没有搞清楚次元扑满是如何模拟出云吟术的…说不定，这是博识学会里的课题，我也是一头雾水。
t_en: Still, I haven't figured out why those Warp Trotters were able to imitate cloudhymn magic... Maybe the scholars in the Intelligentsia Guild can look into this.

t_ch: …地衡司不会还有麻烦事吧？
t_en: ...So, no more troubles in the Realm-Keeping Commission, I guess?

t_ch: 没有了，至少现在是绝对没有了。
t_en: No, at least not for now.

t_ch: 说到底你也不知道隐身的原因？
t_en: And you still don't know why they were invisible, do you?

t_ch: …我也只是推测「隐身现象」与扑满有关，进一步推测，隐身原理与持明族的云吟术相近而已。不然「回响发生装置」就不会生效了。
t_en: ...It's just my hunch, but I think that the "phenomenon of invisibility" is somehow connected to those Trotters. Maybe they used some abilities similar to the Vidyadhara's cloudhymn magic. Otherwise, how would the Reverberation Generator have picked anything up?

t_ch: 毕竟我只是一个地衡司勤务，并不是博识学会的学者，也讲不出什么门门道道……
t_en: But it's just my wild guess. After all, I'm just an orderly of the Realm-Keeping Commission, not a scholar from the Intelligentsia Guild...

t_ch: 总之，我谨代表地衡司上下，感谢您迄今为止的帮助！拿着吧，这是您应得的酬劳。
t_en: Anyway, on behalf of the Commission, I just want to thank you for all your help so far! Here's your well-deserved reward.

t_ch: *电波滤音*不必担心，你先去追其他的扑满吧。它确认安全之后会回来的，大概。
t_en: *electronic voice* Don't worry about it. Track down the other Trotters first. That Trotter will come back once it feels safe again... perhaps.

t_ch: *电波滤音*怎会如此？！
t_en: *electronic voice* How could this be!?

t_ch: *电波滤音*…没想到您竟然放跑了所有的扑满，是故意的，还是不小心？
t_en: *electronic voice* ...I didn't think you would let all the Trotters slip away... Was that intentional, or did it just happen by accident?

t_ch: *电波滤音*…没关系，您先在积玉坊外面等一会儿，它们会回来的。
t_en: *electronic voice* ...Well, don't worry. Just wait outside the Trove of Verdure for a while, and they'll come back soon enough.

t_ch: 又过了一会……
t_en: A few moments later...

t_ch: *电波滤音*请继续狩猎！加油！
t_en: *electronic voice* Keep hunting! You will get it!

t_ch: 哎…队长，迴星港什么时候才能复工复产啊？很多工人都靠这个生活呢，一直封锁着也不是个事儿啊……
t_en: Ah... Captain, when will the Stargazer Navalia resume operations? A lot of workers rely on it to make a living. Keeping it blocked off all the time isn't a solution...

t_ch: 谁不急啊，我也急。不止是工人，现在迴星港封锁，罗浮上的物流系统也会大面积停摆，多耽搁一天都是几千万的损失……
t_en: Everyone's antsy. I'm anxious, too. It's not just about the workers. With the Stargazer Navalia sealed, the logistics system on the Luofu will also come to a massive halt. A single day's delay costs tens of millions in losses...

t_ch: 可咱们现在确实抽调不出那么多人手了啊！要是谁能有这个本事，一个人冲进去清理污染，还能全身而退……
t_en: I get that we can't spare that many people right now! If only someone had the ability to rush in alone, clean up the contamination, and make it back out in one piece...

t_ch: 得了吧你。别做梦了，想点儿实际的吧。
t_en: Alright, you. Quit dreaming. Let's think more realistically, huh?

t_ch: 我能帮得上忙吗？
t_en: Can I help?

t_ch: …哎，这不是那位英雄吗…队长，您还说我做梦呢，救星这不就来了吗！
t_en: ...Hey! Isn't this the hero? Captain, do you still think I'm dreaming? Our savior has come!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！您要是肯来帮忙，那我们可真是得救了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}! If you are willing to help, then we really are saved.

t_ch: 事情是这样的…迴星港必须尽快恢复生产，但现在里面又涌现出了很多建木污染的区域，不得不继续封锁。
t_en: Here's the thing... Stargazer Navalia has to resume production as quickly as possible, but several areas with Ambrosial Arbor pollution have emerged, so we have no other choice but to continue blocking it off.

t_ch: 可是，现在各部门都难以分出人手，事情又相当紧急…所以如果您能去一趟的话……
t_en: But every department is finding it difficult to spare staff members, but it's an urgent matter... So, if you are able to go in...

t_ch: 小事，包我身上了。
t_en: No sweat. I'll handle it.

t_ch: 太好了，谢谢英雄，谢谢英雄。
t_en: Great! Thank you so much!

t_ch: 给钱就行。
t_en: All you need to do is give me some money.

t_ch: 您这话说得就见外了。您是大英雄，怎么能亏待呢？
t_en: Don't mention it. You're the great hero and you deserve it.

t_ch: 不过，我得提醒您一句。在这些污染区域里，怪物之间都被一种特殊的力量连接在一起，牵一发而动全身。
t_en: Let me just remind you that the monsters in these polluted areas are connected by some kind of special power. One slip up will alert all of them to your presence.

t_ch: 您可以理解为，到处都是看不见的线。一旦踩到那些线，那些怪物就会蜂拥而至，让您陷入苦战。
t_en: Imagine it this way. There are invisible wires everywhere. As soon as you step on one of these wires, those monsters will come rushing in together, forcing you into a tough battle.

t_ch: 不过，虽然我们现在还没搞清楚这些怪物连接在一起的原理，但还是能探测它的位置。
t_en: While we currently can't determine the underlying principle of how the monsters are connected together, we can detect their locations.

t_ch: 请您拿着这只谛听吧。（递给玩家）这是工造司改造过的。只要活用这只谛听，就能看到那些看不见的线，从而避免无意义的战斗了。
t_en: Please take this Diting. (Hands to player.) This was modified by the Artisanship Commission. You will be able to see those invisible wires by using this Diting. That'll allow you to avoid any unnecessary battles.

t_ch: 祝您好运。请一定要平安归来。云骑常胜！
t_en: Good luck. Please come back safely. Glory to the Cloud Knights!

t_ch: 这里似乎没有污染。那些怪物就交给云骑来清理吧。不过，还是要当心不要踩到那些「看不见的线」。
t_en: There's no pollution here. Leave the monsters to the Cloud Knights. Still, be careful not to trip on any of those "invisible wires."

t_ch: 太好了，这样应该就能让迴星港更快地恢复生产了。
t_en: Great. This should help Stargazer Navalia resume production quickly.

t_ch: 英雄，你真的是英雄啊，英雄啊！
t_en: You truly are a hero! A living, breathing hero!

t_ch: 您这话说得就见外了。您是大英雄，怎么能亏待呢？
t_en: Don't mention it. You're the great hero and you deserve it.

t_ch: …哈哈，他这人爱激动，你别见怪。请收下这些微不足道的谢礼吧，这是您应得的，英雄。
t_en: ...Haha. Don't mind him. He just likes to get worked up. Please take this small token of our appreciation. You deserve it, hero.

t_ch: 拜拜~
t_en: Bye-bye~

t_ch: 下次再一起玩哦~
t_en: Let's play again sometime!

t_ch: 有点舍不得你…
t_en: I'm gonna miss you a little...

t_ch: 喔哦，这就是迪斯科吗~
t_en: Ooh, so this is disco?

t_ch: 如果交换一下框体…
t_en: What if we swapped frames...

t_ch: 在节拍里寻找浪漫！
t_en: Find romance in the rhythm!

t_ch: 尘灵也可以成为舞蹈家吗？
t_en: Can wispae become dancers too?

t_ch: 轮回的不是五谷，而是情绪…
t_en: It is not the five grains that are stuck in cycles, but emotions...

t_ch: 你好呀~
t_en: Hello~

t_ch: 欢迎来到二次元的世界！
t_en: Welcome to the 2D world!

t_ch: 今天你吃了吗？要好好吃饭哦。
t_en: Did you eat today? Make sure to have a proper meal.

t_ch: 你也是无名客吗？欢迎欢迎！
t_en: Are you a Nameless too? Welcome, welcome!

t_ch: 哦，我亲爱的老伙计！
t_en: Oh, my dear old friend!

t_ch: 这里的风景别有洞天~
t_en: This place has views you wouldn't believe~

t_ch: 绿色能量可恢复所有跳跃点数。
t_en: Green Energy recovers all jump points.

t_ch: 嗯？嗯？？发生什么了？？？
t_en: Huh? Huh?? What's happening???

t_ch: 工兵的工作：按下，然后爆炸。
t_en: A sapper's job: Push the button, then kaboom.

t_ch: 长按「下」+「跳跃」，可以穿过平台哦。
t_en: {LAYOUT_MOBILE#Tap}{LAYOUT_CONTROLLER#Press}{LAYOUT_KEYBOARD#Click} and hold "Down" and "Jump" to pass through platforms.

t_ch: 快试试：无名客勇敢三级跳！
t_en: Go ahead, give it a shot: The Nameless' Brave Triple Jump!

t_ch: 跳得真棒！你简直是个弹簧~
t_en: Great jump! You're basically a spring~

t_ch: 书籍是幻造种进步的阶梯。
t_en: Books are the ladder of progress for imagenae.

t_ch: 瞄准了就射一箭！你懂的。
t_en: Aim and fire an arrow! You know the drill.

t_ch: 比比谁划得快！呃，我就算了。
t_en: Let's see who can paddle the fastest! Uh, except for me, of course.

t_ch: 来吧，挥洒你热爱篮球的汗水！
t_en: Come on! Work up a sweat for the love of basketball!

t_ch: 请、请一口气滑过去…
t_en: P—Please, slide through in one go...

t_ch: 冲啊！持球超时要违例的！
t_en: Charge! Don't hold the ball too long, that's a violation!

t_ch: 金币！我要用金币换整个星球！
t_en: Gold Coins! I'm gonna trade Gold Coins for the entire planet!

t_ch: 每个人都有可能是爱神使者~
t_en: Anyone could be a messenger for the God of Love~

t_ch: 我快乐吗？尘灵不明白这些。
t_en: Am I happy? Wispae do not understand such things.

t_ch: 这些悲欢离合的伊摩吉…顺序上…
t_en: These emotis of joys and sorrows... in the order...

t_ch: 尘灵懂个啥？都是小傻瓜罢了~
t_en: What do wispae know? They're all just little dummies~

t_ch: 要是哪天能晋升幻造种就好了。
t_en: If only I could ascend into an imagenae one day.

t_ch: 得得得，到底哪个是真的？
t_en: Alright, alright, which one is actually real?

t_ch: 你们撞在一起，会湮灭成光吗？
t_en: If you crash into each other, will you obliterate into a flash of light?

t_ch: 老板！我举报有人翘班！
t_en: Boss! I wanna report someone for ditching work!

t_ch: 往上去是不是要改下传送方向？
t_en: If we want to go up, do we need to change the teleport direction?

t_ch: 瞄准了，把爱心射下来！
t_en: Aimed and locked! Shoot down that heart!

t_ch: 谁有马桶搋子？借我用用！
t_en: Who's got a plunger? Let me borrow it real quick!

t_ch: 有没有一种坐星穹列车的感觉？
t_en: Does it feel like you're on the Astral Express?

t_ch: 懂了，吃金币也能自动战斗。
t_en: Got it. Collecting Gold Coins triggers auto-battle too.

t_ch: 你怎么穿着火花做的衣服啊？
t_en: Why are you wearing clothes made by Sparxie?

t_ch: 愚者的喜剧，本质还是金币呀~
t_en: The Fool's comedy... at its core, it's really just about Gold Coins~

t_ch: 前方注意，本单位即将发射！
t_en: Heads up, this unit is about to fire!

t_ch: 好酷，再来一次！
t_en: So cool! Do it again!

t_ch: 已成功着陆，屁股急需治疗！
t_en: Successfully touched down... My butt needs urgent medical attention!

t_ch: 嘿！你宝箱忘拿了~
t_en: Hey! You forgot your treasure chest~

t_ch: 你们想升高时，就向上仰望。我向下俯视，因为我已经升高。
t_en: When you wish to rise, look upward. I look down because I've already risen above.

t_ch: 广告牌上的角色和尘灵有什么区别？他们也不过是在你注视的目光中给予你固定不变的反馈。
t_en: What's the difference between characters on a billboard and wispae? They're both just giving you the same canned responses when you look at them.

t_ch: 瞧，噗噜的未来世界。一切都落进水里，却不再有什么落进深深的井底。
t_en: Behold, future world of ploos. Everything has fallen into the water, yet nothing falls into the deep bottom of the well anymore.

t_ch: 天下熙熙，皆为利来；天下攘攘，皆为利往。
t_en: All the hustle and bustle of the world is driven by profit. All the coming and going in the world is for gain.

t_ch: 你是一座大桥，我是一座小桥。我们是桥梁而不是目的，是过渡和没落，而不是虚幻的月亮。
t_en: You're a grand bridge, I'm a humble one. We're bridges, not destinations. We are transitions and declines, not some illusory moon.

t_ch: 从前我也幻想有一座独栋的房子，只有我自己住在里面——直到我意识到家务劳动是多么沉重的一种负担。
t_en: I used to dream about having a detached house all to myself... until I realized what a massive burden housework is.

t_ch: 如果在世界的中心呼唤闪电，我会因为装模做样而被天雷惩罚吗？
t_en: If I beckoned lightning at the center of the world, would I get struck by divine lightning for being pretentious?

t_ch: 铁打的座位，流水的尘灵…我们都是失败者，我们也都是成功者。
t_en: The seats stay the same, but the wispae keep changing... We're all losers, and we're all winners.

t_ch: 同路的时光虽然短暂，留下的记忆却可以绵长。银河漫漫，你我都是彼此旅途中的一站。
t_en: Though our time together was brief, the memories we made will last forever. Across this vast cosmos, we're just a stop along each other's journey.

t_ch: 如果所有的列车都只抵达同样的终点站，我们对旅行的看法会发生变化吗？
t_en: If all express trains only ever reached the same final destination, would our view on traveling change?

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#YOU SHALL NOT PASS}此路不通{RUBY_E#}」——它可以是英雄的豪言壮语，也可以是一个尘灵对您的温馨提示。
t_en: "{RUBY_B#YOU SHALL NOT PASS}Road Closed{RUBY_E#}": It could be a hero's rallying cry, or just a wispae giving you a friendly hint.

t_ch: 空心的铁皮人梦想着找到一颗心…在故事的开头，他们总是很难察觉，自己并不需要模仿人类。
t_en: The hollow Tin Man dreamed of finding a heart... At the start of the story, they always find it hard to realize they don't need to imitate humans.

t_ch: 不知道在新年假期里，门神会不会也有加班费？
t_en: I wonder if the deities of the gate get overtime pay during the New Year holidays, too?

t_ch: 有些人不必看，有些人看不懂——菜单的真正作用，是让你觉得自己仿佛有的选。
t_en: Some people don't need to look, and some others wouldn't get it anyway... The real purpose of a menu is to make you think you actually have a choice.

t_ch: 在别人眼中，我们都不过是一个被捏扁搓圆的等差序列…拿走我吧，表情并没有意义。
t_en: In everyone's eyes, we're nothing but an arithmetic sequence to be squished and molded however they please... Just take me. Expressions are unnecessary.

t_ch: 你站在桥上看风景，看风景的人在楼上看你。
t_en: You're watching the scenery as you stand upon the bridge, while someone enjoying the view upstairs is watching you.

t_ch: 鸟粪石是最高品质的磷矿。如果我富含磷的成分…是否也可以靠磷火来成为一只萤火虫呢？
t_en: Guano is the highest quality phosphate ore. If I were rich in phosphorus... could I also become a firefly through phosphorescent fire?

t_ch: 如果有什么无法实现的愿望，那就去航海吧。在星海的彼岸，人或许能重新找回自己的面容。
t_en: If there's a wish that can't come true, then sail out into the ocean. Beyond the sea of stars, perhaps one can find their true selves again.

t_ch: 生活不会给你贴超速罚单——它知道你最大的困境是点不着火花塞。
t_en: Life won't give you a speeding ticket. It knows your biggest struggle is getting your spark plugs to fire in the first place.

t_ch: 写字的人不知道自己在写什么是一种常态。如果他们知道，他们就不会写那么多了。
t_en: Writers not knowing what they're writing about? That's just par for the course. If they actually knew, they wouldn't churn out nearly as much.

t_ch: 生命的意义，是漫长低谷中那唯一的高峰。当那个机会到来的时候，请记得将生死置之度外。
t_en: The meaning of life? It's that one peak in an endless valley of lows. When that moment comes knocking, remember to throw caution to the wind and forget about life and death.

t_ch: 自从人们穿上了鞋子，他们的脚就不能感受大地的温度。自从人们穿上了一样的鞋子，他们的脚就再也不属于他们自己。
t_en: Ever since people started wearing shoes, their feet can no longer feel the warmth of the lands. Ever since people started wearing the same shoes, their feet no longer belong to themselves.

t_ch: 如果你走上了岔路…不要悲伤，不要心急：岔路里才会有无法预期的风景。
t_en: If you find yourself on a detour... don't be sad, don't be anxious: It's on the detours that you'll find the most unexpected scenery.

t_ch: 你有没有发现一件怪事？我们明明是「无名尘灵」，却拥有天罡地煞的名字。真不知道人们究竟记住了我们，还是没记住我们……
t_en: Ever noticed something weird? We're called "Nameless Wispae," yet we've got these grand divine names. Makes you wonder if people actually remember us or not...

t_ch: 如果我们把时间切成孤立的瞬间，飞行的箭矢也可以同时静止不动。电子屏幕有刷新率的上限…宇宙也会如此吗？
t_en: If we slice time into isolated moments, a flying arrow can be both in motion and perfectly still. Electronic screens have refresh rate limits... could the universe work the same way?

t_ch: 小隐隐于地，大隐隐于天。
t_en: The lesser hermit hides on earth, the greater hermit hides amongst the skies.

t_ch: 人生五十年，与下天长久相较，如梦又似幻…才怪哩。现在这生活方式，每十年一大变，五十年长到不行了好吧。
t_en: A human life span of fifty years, compared to the span of the heavens, is but a fleeting dream... Yeah, right. With the way we live now, everything changes every decade. Fifty years feels like an absolute eternity, okay?

t_ch: 人生在世，不过是在试图配平一个永远也配不平的等式。
t_en: Life's just one big attempt at balancing an equation that'll never balance out.

t_ch: 升起吧，伟大的正午！让我做全世界的日光灯。
t_en: Rise, o high noon! Let me be the fluorescent light of the entire world.

t_ch: 这世界上最深重的孤独，是你光天化日之下却必须打着手电筒——而且在人群里找不到人。
t_en: The deepest loneliness in this world is when you're standing in broad daylight but still need to carry a flashlight. Even then, you can't find someone in the crowd.

t_ch: 可别笑话仓鼠球——上班下班，上学放学，谁又不是生活在仓鼠球里面呢？
t_en: Don't go laughing at the hamster wheels. Commuting back and forth from work or school, aren't we all just living in hamster wheels of our own?

t_ch: 当你找回了自己的鞋子，你也就找回了自己的道路。
t_en: When you find your shoes, you'll find your way.

t_ch: 黑板需要板擦，人类需要睡眠，有毒的记忆需要遗忘。
t_en: Blackboards need erasers, humans need sleep, and toxic memories need to be forgotten.

t_ch: 如果冬天永远不会到来，那么永恒的秋天会比短促的春天得到更高的评价吗？
t_en: If winter never came, would an eternal fall be rated higher than a fleeting spring?

t_ch: 一个残酷的事实——如果没有在风浪里晕船到吐，你就没有真的认识过大海。
t_en: Unpopular opinion: If you haven't hurled your guts out from seasickness in rough waves, you haven't truly met the ocean.

t_ch: 如果整个世界都只有黑白，你永远也不会知道自己其实拥有色彩。
t_en: If the whole world were just black and white, you'd never know you had colors.

t_ch: 世上的很多事，都好像一块屏幕——屏幕里面的想出去，屏幕外面的想进来。
t_en: A lot of things in this world are like a screen: Those inside want to get out, and those outside want to get in.

t_ch: 如何判断自己是不是被困在了一个被演算模拟的世界？找找身边的镜子，看看它们反不反光，能不能映照出无穷个你和无穷个她。
t_en: How can you tell if you're trapped in a simulated world built by extrapolation? Look for mirrors around you and see if they actually reflect, if they can show infinite versions of you and infinite versions of her.

t_ch: 什么是银河里最厉害的功夫？第一流的剑客，他的锋刃并不用于斩人，却要用于裁纸。
t_en: What's the most powerful martial art in the cosmos? A first-rate swordsman doesn't use their blade to cut down foes, but instead use it to cut paper.

t_ch: 用流在外面的泪，你写不成一本好书。用流在里面的血才可以。
t_en: A masterpiece is not written with tears wept for the world to see. It is written with the blood that flows within.

t_ch: 当诚实成为快乐的先决条件，你和我就可以天下无敌。请收下这条捷径。
t_en: When honesty becomes the prerequisite for happiness, you and I can be unstoppable. Here, take this shortcut.

t_ch: 水中的月亮和天上的月亮，哪个才是更真实的月亮？先别笑，这不是一个天文学的问题。
t_en: The moon reflected in the water or the moon in the sky, which one's the real deal? Hold your laughter, this isn't an astronomy question.

t_ch: 人生中难免会遇到一些问题，一旦答错就意味着死亡。提问，唯一一个无法和欢愉兼容的命途是什么？
t_en: Life inevitably throws questions at you. Get one wrong, and it spells death. Here's a question: What's the one and only Path that's incompatible with Elation?

t_ch: ——答案是毁灭。您猜对了吗？
t_en: The answer is the Destruction. Did you guess it right?

t_ch: 如果你发现一群陌生人又很看重你，说话又好听——快跑，别掉进了商业广告的陷阱。
t_en: If you run into a bunch of strangers who think the world of you and sweet-talk you excessively, run. Don't fall for their marketing trap.

t_ch: 我在水面的这头，你在水面的那头。究竟是谁在水面之上，又是谁在水面之下？啊，我忘记了你们人类不能在水下呼吸。
t_en: I'm on this side of the water, you're on the other. But which of us is above the water, and which is below? Oh, that's right... I forgot you humans can't breathe underwater.

t_ch: 为什么电影里喜欢拍大侠在屋顶打架？因为现实中既没有大侠，也不会有人真的在屋顶打架。电影，很奇妙吧~
t_en: What is with the obsession with sword masters clashing atop roof tiles in movies? In reality, there are no heroes, and nobody actually fights on roofs. Movies are a curious thing, aren't they?

t_ch: 如果你是要寻找水中的幻月，那么你可就走错路啦。唔…这句话是我第几次说了来着？其实我本来的台词并不是这个，可惜愚者们修改了我的剧本——开个玩笑，我的台词一开始就长这样。
t_en: Looking for the Phantasmoon in the reflection? Sorry, you're in the wrong place. Wait... how many times have I said that? My script wasn't even supposed to say this, but the Fools messed with the script... Just kidding! This was always the line.

t_ch: 如果自动售货机不出货，那就麻烦你踢它一脚。曾经有个尘灵在这里提醒人们「就算被吞钱了也不要踢一脚哦」——结果他们每个人都要踢两脚。
t_en: If the vending machine doesn't dispense, then please give it a kick. There used to be a wispae here reminding people to not kick it even if it eats your money... So naturally, everyone kicked it twice.

t_ch: 请抬头看一看，并且，千万不要抬头看。有个数学家告诉我，这样说可以让一个人抬头看的机率最大化。
t_en: Look up, but whatever you do, don't look up. A mathematician told me that saying it like this maximizes the chances of getting someone to look up.

t_ch: 在侦探小说里，这样的一个冰柜很可能隐藏着惊天的秘密。但很可惜，住在这里的人说了，冰柜里只有速冻食品。顺便一提，我的这两句台词是他自己帮忙写的。
t_en: A freezer like this is usually where the shocking twist is kept in mystery novels. Sadly, the resident claims there's nothing inside but frozen food. Incidentally, he's the one who helped me write that bit.

t_ch: 如果睡眠是死亡的预演，那么失眠又是什么？
t_en: If sleep is a rehearsal for death, then what does it make insomniacs?

t_ch: 失眠是生活无意义的注脚。
t_en: Insomnia is life's footnote on meaninglessness.

t_ch: 啊，被你发现啦。可以帮忙把我藏进一个黑洞里吗？这样或许就能摆脱愚者们的傻笑了。
t_en: Ah, you found me. Could you help hiding me in a black hole? Maybe that way I can escape those Fools and their annoying grins.

t_ch: 人生就像银河里的星轨，总会在意想不到的地方发现新的道岔。
t_en: Life is like the star rails in the cosmos, you'll always find new detours in the most unexpected places.

t_ch: 在酒馆里待久了，你会误以为世界是静止的——愚者们日复一日，总是在毫无差别的情绪氛围里做着毫无差别的事情。
t_en: Spend enough time in a tavern, and you'll start thinking the world's stuck on repeat. Fools doing the same old thing, day in and day out, wrapped up in the same bland emotions.

t_ch: 分不清左右手和分不清上下方哪个更可怕一点？别问我，我既没有手也没有脚。
t_en: Which is scarier: Not knowing your left from your right, or not knowing up from down? Don't ask me, I've got neither hands nor feet.

t_ch: 有些尘灵拿龟兔赛跑的故事去笑话兔子。它们似乎不明白，我们既像乌龟一样慢，又像兔子一样懒。
t_en: Some wispae like to mock the hare in the story of the tortoise and the hare. They don't seem to get it. We've got the tortoise's speed and the hare's work ethics.

t_ch: 悄悄告诉你一个秘密——医生并没有能力把你洗脑，但是手机上的视频可以。
t_en: Let me tell you a little secret: Doctors don't actually have the ability to brainwash you, but videos on your phone? Totally can.

t_ch: 为什么愚者们如此喜欢厕所笑话？别担心，他们只是无差别地嘲笑一切自己无法消化的东西。就算是阿哈本人来了，那也得被塞进这里冲走。
t_en: Why do Fools love toilet humor so much? Don't worry, they just indiscriminately laugh at everything they can't digest. Even if Aha THEMSELF showed up, THEY'd get stuffed in here and flushed away too.

t_ch: 这年头的愚者们认为舞台上的角色都是小丑…却没有发觉他们自己变成了一群没有灵魂的小丑。
t_en: These days, Fools think all the characters on stage are clowns... never realizing they've become a bunch of soulless clowns themselves.

t_ch: 嘘…愚者们正坐在一个火山口上，哪怕从满月的那头都能看到这里汹涌的岩浆……
t_en: Shhh... The Fools are sitting on top of a volcano, even when we can see the raging magma from the furthest end of the full moon...

t_ch: 踩我！
t_en: Step on me!

t_ch: 这里出发！
t_en: Let's go!

t_ch: 终点在望！加油！
t_en: The finish line's in sight! Keep it up!

t_ch: 没错，这里就是终点！
t_en: That's right, this is the end of the line!

t_ch: 哎呀！小心别摔跤哦？
t_en: Whoa there! Watch your step!

t_ch: 小心！有陷阱！
t_en: Watch out! It's a trap!

t_ch: 一路顺风！
t_en: Safe travels!

t_ch: 胜利在望！
t_en: Victory is within reach!

t_ch: 好技术！
t_en: Nice moves!

t_ch: 我要留个纪念！
t_en: I'm keeping this as a souvenir!

t_ch: 画画好难啊…画画比数学都难…
t_en: Drawing is so hard... It's even harder than math...

t_ch: 哎呀…这下被你发现了。
t_en: Uh-oh... looks like you found me out.

t_ch: 你得先这样再这样，然后那样…
t_en: You gotta do this first, then that, and then that...

t_ch: 哼哼，这是对观察力的考验。
t_en: Hehe, this is a test of observation skills.

t_ch: 这里可以休息的哦~
t_en: You can take a break here, you know~

t_ch: 左边的是花多芬吗？
t_en: Is that Florthoven there on the left?
